data.title: Halliburton extends winning streak, shares rise for seventh consecutive session
data.description:
Shares of Halliburton (NYSE:HAL) climbed 2.27% to $25.22 on Friday, marking its seventh straight session of gains.

The oilfield services provider has gained momentum in recent days, posting steady gains over the past week. The biggest gain was on September 23, when the stock jumped 7.34%. Over the past month, shares have risen 14.99%, but the stock is down -6.07% year-to-date.

With the recent surge, Halliburton is up significantly from recent lows and continues to build momentum, though broader investor sentiment remains mixed.

According to Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating, HAL carries a Hold recommendation, with an average score of 2.88 out of 5. The company earned a B+ in profitability, but lower marks in other categories including C- in valuation, C in momentum, D- in growth, and D+ in revisions, showing cautious optimism from the quantitative models.

Seeking Alpha analysts also remain cautious, maintaining a Hold rating overall.

However, Wall Street sentiment skews more bullish. Twelve analysts rate the stock a Strong Buy, while five recommend Buy. Eight analysts suggest holding, and notably, none currently recommend selling the stock.

The recent gain was buoyed by higher oil prices that lifted sentiment across the energy sector and renewed investor interest in oilfield services stocks, boosting the company's market value and signaling growing confidence in near-term demand.

In early September, the company had announced workforce cuts amid weaker oilfield activity and falling crude prices. Halliburton also reported a sharp profit decline in the second quarter, highlighting ongoing challenges.

Despite the recent gain, shares remain down for the year, reflecting ongoing uncertainty in the energy sector. An analyst pointed out, “We might be on the verge of a resurgence in drilling activities,” adding that Halliburton is “well-positioned for a potential boom in US drilling” driven by geopolitical and policy shifts.

data.title: Catalyst Watch: September Jobs Report, Nike earnings, Amazon hardware event, Fermi IPO, and the Sony spinoff
data.description:
Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

 

Monday - September 29

 

  • Volatility watch -  Bitmine Immersion Technologies (BMNR) and IREN (IREN) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Tourmaline Bio (TRML) and Churchill Capital Corp. X (CCCX). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Oxford Square (OXSQ) and Sprouts Farmers (SFM).
  • IPO watch - Knorex (KNRX), Neptune Insurance (NP), and Fermi (FRMI) are expected to price their IPOs and begin trading during the week. Fermi has attracted a significant amount of attention due to its plans to build massive power plants to provide electricity to AI data centers. The quiet period expires on Fitness Champs (FCHL) to free up analysts to post ratings. The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Waton Financial (WTF) and AsiaStrategy (SORA).
  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Carnival (CCL), Jefferies Financial (JEF), and Vail Resorts (MTN).
  • All day - Notable investor events include TD Bank Group's (TD) Investor Day, ArcBest's (ARCB) Investor Day, and TotalEnergies' (TTE) Investor Day.
  • All day - Sony Group (NYSE:SONY) will spin off its financial services arm, Sony Financial Group, in a major restructuring move. More than 80% of Sony Financial shares will be distributed to Sony shareholders via a dividend-in-kind.
  • All day - FedEx (FDX) will hold its annual meeting. Investors will be looking for news of the business split scheduled for next year.
  • Federal Reserve watch - It is a busy day of Fedspeak with Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller, St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem, and New York Federal Reserve Bank President John Williams due to talk.

 

Tuesday - September 30

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Nike (NYSE:NKE), Paychex (PAYX), Lamb Weston (LW), and United Natural Foods (UNFI).
  • Federal Reserve watch - It is another active day of Fedspeak with Federal Reserve Vice Chair Philip Jefferson, Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan, and Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee all due to talk publicly.
  • All day - Amazon (NASDAQ:AMZN) will hold a hardware reveal event. The showcase is expected to spotlight a new wave of Echo smart speakers, Fire TV devices, and Kindle e-readers, all designed to leverage the upgraded Alexa Plus AI assistant.
  • 9:00 a.m. Economists have the release of the S&P/Case-Shiller House Price Index circled as a key update for the housing market.
  • 1:30 p.m. Chicago Federal Reserve Bank President Austan Goolsbee will participate in a moderated question-and-answer session before the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago Midwest Agriculture Conference.

 

Wednesday - October 1

 

  • Earnings watch - Notable companies due to report include Conagra Brands (CAG), and Cal-Maine Foods (CALM).
  • Data watch - Key data reports due to arrive during the first few days of the week include updates on deliveries from Tesla (TSLA), Nio (NIO), XPeng (XPEV), Rivian Automotive (RIVN), Li Auto (LI), ZEEKR Intelligent (ZK), Polestar Automotive (PSNY), and Lucid Group (LCID). Quarterly sales reports from General Motors (GM), Ford Motor (F), and Toyota (TM) will also be closely watched. Other reports of high interest to investors will be the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release, the monthly update on Class 8 truck orders, firearm background check data from the FBI, and discount broker DARTs reports.
  • All day - Google (GOOG) (GOOGL) is holding a Gemini event that is expected to unveil the integration of Gemini AI for smart home devices, marking a major upgrade from Google Assistant across Nest Cam, smart speakers, and doorbells. New hardware devices could be announced.
  • All day - Golden Week begins in China to commemorate the 75th anniversary of the founding of the People’s Republic of China. The holiday is especially important to the Macau casino sector, including Wynn Resorts (WYNN), Las Vegas Sands (LVS), MGM Resorts (MGM), and Melco Resorts (MLCO).

 

Thursday - October 2

 

  • All day - The Federal Reserve will issue its latest balance sheet report.
  • 10:00 a.m. The U.S. Factory Orders report will be released by the Census Bureau.
  • 10:30 a.m. Dallas Federal Reserve Bank President Lorie Logan will participate in a moderated conversation before the UT Evolving Energy and Policy Landscape Conference.

 

Friday - October 3

 

  • All day - The United Nations will release its latest FAO food price index and monthly grains report, which are watched by commodities traders.
  • All day - European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde, Bank of Japan Governor Kazuo Ueda, and Bank of England Governor Bailey are all scheduled to give talks.
  • 8:30 a.m. The September Jobs Report will be released. Economists expect a weak tally of 50K job additions and the unemployment rate to hold steady at 4.3%.

 

Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this story

data.title: Electronic Arts jumps on report of $50 billion deal to go private
data.description:
Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) rose 15% after a report that the video game maker is nearing a deal to go private. 

A group of investors, including private-equity firms Silver Lake and Saudi Arabia's Public Investment Fund, may announce a deal for Electronic Arts (NASDAQ:EA) as soon as next week, according to a WSJ report, which cited people familiar with the matter. EA may be valued at as much as $50 billion in a deal.

Developing story ...

data.title: ING Group slashes Russian offshore exposure by 85% since 2022 exit
data.description:
ING Group (NYSE:ING) has slashed its offshore exposure to Russian clients by more than 85% to €0.7B ($819.1M) since its market exit three years ago, the European lender said on Friday.

Since February 2022, the bank has taken on no new business with Russian firms, it said, noting it has also scaled down operations and has taken actions to separate our business in Russia from ING’s (NYSE:ING) networks and systems.

"We will continue to further reduce our offshore exposure to Russian clients," it said, amid the Russia-Ukraine war.

Also, the proposed sale of ING Bank (Eurasia) JSC to Global Development -- which would end ING's (NYSE:ING) activities in the Russian market -- faces uncertain timing, as the buyer has yet to receive all necessary approvals.

The deal was initially expected to be completed in Q3 2025, but now, "there is currently no realistic prospect of completing the transaction in the third quarter."

The sale is expected to have a negative impact of around 7 basis points on ING’s (ING) common equity tier 1 (CET1) ratio.

ING shares gained 1.5% in midafternoon trading in the U.S.

data.title: ONE Gas upgraded at Mizuho, seen poised to recapture historical premium
data.description:
ONE Gas (NYSE:OGS) +2.7% in Friday's trading as Mizuho upgraded the utility to Outperform from Neutral with an $86 price target, raised from $77, seeing "several reasons to be constructive,
including Texas HB 4384 legislation benefits, upcoming regulatory catalysts, and interest
rate tailwinds."

ONE Gas (NYSE:OGS) shares trade at a modest discount to gas utility peers despite being a fully-regulated utility with a strong balance sheet, and historically traded at a premium before restating its guidance outlook in 2023-24, Mizuho analyst Gabriel Moreen said, believing the premium should return as "recent and forthcoming tailwinds... essentially rehabilitate the prior earnings trajectory before OGS went through its restatement process."

Past headwinds driven by higher interest rates, O&M inflation, and commodity volatility have been mitigated through regulatory updates and improving macroeconomic conditions, Moreen said, now expecting ONE Gas (OGS) can deliver at the higher end of its growth outlook.

ONE Gas (OGS) is "a fully regulated utility in supportive jurisdictions with solid customer growth and a healthy balance sheet,"  Moreen wrote, anticipating that Wall Street will catch up with expectations as more clarification is provided on Texas legislation benefits.

data.title: STAAR Surgical pushes for all-cash merger with Alcon
data.description:
• STAAR Surgical Company (NASDAQ:STAA) said in a presentation today that its $28.00 per share all-cash merger with Alcon provides its stockholders compelling, certain, cash value at a significant premium across multiple measures.
  • It represents a 51% premium to STAAR’s closing stock price on August 4, 2025.
  • STAAR’s second largest active stockholder, Soleus Capital Master is supportive of the merger and intends to vote in favor absent a material change in circumstances.
  • “If the Alcon merger does not move forward, we believe STAAR’s valuation will fall substantially, exposing stockholders to significant risk. The day prior to the announcement of the Alcon merger, STAAR’s stock closed at $18.49 per share following a more than 50% stock price decline in the prior year,"  Stephen Farrell, CEO of STAAR, said,
  • STAA is +0.299065% to $26.83
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Sinclair brings back Jimmy Kimmel's show days after Disney cancels his suspension
data.description:
Sinclair Inc. (NASDAQ:SBGI) said Friday it will reinstate Jimmy Kimmel Live! on its ABC-affiliated TV stations, just three days after media and entertainment giant Disney (NYSE:DIS) revoked the comedian's suspension in the wake of his remarks on slain political activist Charlie Kirk.

"Over the last week, we have received thoughtful feedback from viewers, advertisers, and community leaders representing a wide range of perspectives," Sinclair said in a statement. "We have also witnessed troubling acts of violence, including the despicable incident of a shooting at an ABC affiliate station in Sacramento. These events underscore why responsible broadcasting matters and why respectful dialogue between differing voices remains so important."

On Wednesday, rival TV station company Nexstar (NASDAQ:NXST) said it held "productive" talks with Disney (NYSE:DIS) regarding Kimmel's show but continued to preempt it on its ABC-affiliated networks.

Sinclair owns more ABC-affiliated stations in the U.S., totaling about 40, while Nexstar, being the largest local TV station in the country, owns about 30.

Kimmel's suspension lasted only for six days after advocates from both sides of the political aisle disagreed with the move. Disney also witnessed a wave of subscription cancellations by users who viewed the action against the comedian to be harsh and as an attack on free speech.

data.title: Big Pharma rebounds despite Trump’s latest tariff threat
data.description:
Leading global drugmakers traded higher on Friday as Wall Street digested President Donald Trump’s latest tariff announcement targeted at brand-name pharmaceuticals made by companies without any ongoing U.S. investments.

“Starting October 1st, 2025, we will be imposing a 100% Tariff on any branded or patented Pharmaceutical Product, unless a Company IS BUILDING their Pharmaceutical Manufacturing Plant in America,” Trump said in a social media post late Thursday.

While shares of Japanese and EU drugmakers came under pressure after the announcement, the VanEck Vectors Pharmaceutical ETF (NASDAQ:PPH), which represents 25 leading global pharma stocks, added ~1%, ending a five-day decline thanks mainly to gains in U.S. stocks.

Notable gainers included Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ), Eli Lilly (NYSE:LLY), Merck (NYSE:MRK), and Bristol Myers (BMY), all of which have declared billions of dollars of investments to expand their U.S. manufacturing footprint.

In reaction, Citi argued that the announcement “lifts a significant overhang, as political uncertainties have kept investor interest at bay for most of this year.” As recently as August, Trump said that his administration is prepared to impose levies of up to 250% on pharmaceuticals, a significant step-up from the 200% pharma tariffs he indicated only a few weeks ago.

Analyst Geoff Meacham called the news a relief to biopharma investors, “given the preponderance of large caps in the sector have already announced significant investments since the major tariff announcement and will therefore not be impacted.”

“When this development is taken into consideration with the Fed’s rate lowering last week—which is posited to generate renewed interest in SMid biotechs—we now see a clearer path ahead again,” Bloomberg News reported, quoting Meacham.

Jefferies analyst Akash Tewari agreed: “We think this is a win for Pharma since several pharma companies are already investing in US manufacturing,” Tewari wrote.

Meanwhile, JPMorgan’s Chris Schott opined that the tariffs will not apply to a majority of pharmaceuticals imported to the U.S., as large-cap pharma companies have already begun or are currently increasing their U.S.-based manufacturing operations. Schott also argued that the group's inventory buildups should lead to no or minimal exposure, “which gives companies time to start manufacturing buildouts, if they haven’t already.”

However, Edmund Ingham, Seeking Alpha Investing Group Leader for Haggerston BioHealth, sounded a neutral tone, noting that another major event awaits leading drugmakers later this month when a 60-day ultimatum imposed by Trump, forcing them to lower U.S. drug prices, is set to end.

“Big Pharma faces uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes MFN drug pricing and 100% tariffs on non-US manufactured patented medicines,” Ingham wrote with a Hold recommendation on PPH. “Investors should remain cautious: while policy volatility threatens valuations, long-term fundamentals and product innovation still drive sector resilience,” he added.

data.title: Private equity owners of MRI Software seek sale or IPO - report
data.description:
The private equity owners of real estate software firm MRI Software are evaluating options for the company, including a possible sale or initial public offering.

Any potential deal for MRI may happen within the next year and value the firm at as much as $10 billion, including debt, according to a Reuters report on Friday, which cited people familiar with the matter.  The ownership consortium includes TA Associates, Harvest Partners and GI Partners.

MRI is working with Goldman Sachs after the software firm received inbound acquisition interest from other real estate and tech firms, according to the report. MRI is nearing $1 billion in annual revenue and it generates EBITDA of $400 million. 

MRI declined to comment to Reuters.

data.title: Apple working on ChatGPT-like app to aid new version of Siri: report
data.description:
• Apple (NASDAQ:AAPL) is working on an internal app, akin to ChatGPT, to help the new version of its voice assistant Siri, set to be released next year, Bloomberg reported.
  • Known as Veritas, the app will not be released publicly and is only for internal use, the news outlet added, citing people familiar with the matter. The app is being used to test and evaluated features of the new version of Siri, including the ability to search through personal data. 
  • In 2023, Apple also worked on an internal ChatGPT-like app, codenamed AppleGPT, which used the AJax generative AI framework.
  • The new version of Siri could be unveiled as soon as March 2026, the news outlet added. The current version of Siri integrates ChatGPT for certain tasks, a feature that was announced in 2024 at Apple's developer conference.
  • Apple did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
